name: Genomic Creature Language — Codon Dictionary
description: Complete mapping of all 64 codons to morphogenetic instructions, named macro expansions, and body plan templates for the GCL in-house design language.

ATG.label: Body axis init
ATG.note: Start codon. Always required. Initializes body axis and head.

CCT.label: Body segment
CCT.note: Proline — kink-former. Models Wnt/Notch segmentation clock.

CCC.label: Body segment
CCC.note: Synonymous with CCT. Same morpheme — silent if swapped.

CCA.label: Body segment
CCA.note: Synonymous with CCT. Same morpheme — silent if swapped.

CCG.label: Body segment
CCG.note: Synonymous with CCT. Same morpheme — silent if swapped.

GCT.label: Segment elongation
GCT.note: Alanine — helix former. Stretches current segment without adding mass.

GTT.label: Bilateral limb pair
GTT.note: Valine — branched side chain. Models Pitx/Dll bilateral limb bud induction. Always creates bilateral (left+right) pair.

TTA.label: Body mass increase
TTA.note: Leucine — large hydrophobic. Increases body width/mass.

ATT.label: Rigidity / joint lock
ATT.note: Isoleucine — rigid branched chain. Reduces joint bend, stiffens limbs.

GGT.label: Articulation joint
GGT.note: Glycine — smallest AA, most flexible. Creates bend in body axis or appendage.

CGT.label: Horn / protrusion
CGT.note: Arginine — guanidinium group, rigid protrusion. Models RXFP2-driven horn growth. Pairwise when early in sequence.

TTT.label: Fin / wing / flap
TTT.note: Phenylalanine — planar aromatic ring. Models flat lateral structures: fins, wings, dewlaps, tail flukes. Context-interpreted by species.

TGG.label: Unique feature
TGG.note: Tryptophan — rarest AA. Models rare developmental innovations: compound eyes, bioluminescence, electroreception, ink glands.

CAT.label: Sensory organ (eye)
CAT.note: Histidine — imidazole ring, pH-sensitive. Models photoreceptor/sensory cell specification. Produces eye pair.

GAT.label: Dorsal spine
GAT.note: Aspartate — negative charge, ridge-former. Models En/Hh-driven dorsal spine or ridge.

GAA.label: Ridge amplifier
GAA.note: Glutamate — longer version of Asp. Increases height of adjacent spine.

AAA.label: Claw / talon tip
AAA.note: Lysine — long positive chain, terminal hook. Added to active branch tips.

TCT.label: Surface texture
TCT.note: Serine — hydroxyl group, surface-active. Drives scale / fur / feather texture rendering.

ACT.label: Color stripe
ACT.note: Threonine — hydroxyl group, stripe/banding. Shifts current pigment hue. Models Wnt-driven stripe patterning.

TGT.label: Structural crosslink
TGT.note: Cysteine — disulfide bonds. Stiffens/hardens current region. Models keratinization (horn, carapace, beak, nail).

TAT.label: Pigment patch
TAT.note: Tyrosine — melanin precursor (real melanin biosynthesis pathway). Lays down a pigment patch. Sequential Tyr = denser pigment.

AAT.label: Soft tissue
AAT.note: Asparagine — N-glycosylation site, cartilage-like region. Produces soft transparent area.

CAA.label: Muscle bulge
CAA.note: Glutamine — glutamate amide, muscle fiber. Bulges the current segment.

TAA.label: Translation stop
TAA.note: Amber stop codon. Ends body plan. Creature truncates here.

#WING_PAIR.description: Bilateral wing pair. Fin→Branch→Fin. Models Dpp-mediated wing disc duplication.

#DEWLAP.description: Colorful throat flap. Fin + 2x pigment. Models anole dewlap: flat tissue + melanin.

#COMPOUND_EYE.description: Insect compound eye. 2x Feature + Sensor. Models Pax6/Dac eye cluster.

#ANTENNA.description: Flexible antenna. 2x Gly joint + Arg tip. Models antennal development via homothorax.

#BIG_HORN.description: Large paired horns. 4x Arg. Models RXFP2-mediated horn growth amplification.

#CURL_HORN.description: Curled spiral horn. Arg-Gly alternating. Joint-breaks create curl geometry.

#CARAPACE.expansion: CCG TGT CCG TGT CCG TGT
#CARAPACE.description: Hard shell carapace. Segment-Crosslink alternating. Models exoskeleton keratinization.

#TAIL.description: Tapered tail ending in STOP. 3x Ala elongation.

#SPOTTED_COAT.description: Spot pattern. Pigment-Joint alternating creates irregular spot distribution.

#STRIPED_COAT.description: Striped coat. Stripe-Pigment alternating creates banded pattern.

#MANE.description: Mane. 3x Texture then 2x Thicken at head region.

#BIOLUMINESCENT.description: Bioluminescent patch. Feature-Pigment-Feature. Models luciferin/luciferase gene cluster.

#PROBOSCIS.description: Proboscis / elongated mouth part. Claw base + 3x elongation.

#DORSAL_FIN.description: Dorsal fin. 2 spines then fin flap.

#SWIM_BLADDER.description: Buoyancy structure. 2x Muscle + SoftTissue = inflatable chamber visual.

#EYESPOT.description: Eyespot pattern: ring pigment around feature. Models butterfly/peacock eyespot (optix gene).

#PLUMAGE.description: Iridescent plumage. Texture + Stripe hue-shift + Pigment. Models nanostructure-based iridescence.

#BEAK.description: Keratinized beak. Claw + Crosslink at head position.

#WEBBED_FOOT.description: Webbed foot. Branch + SoftTissue + Fin = webbing between digits.

#SUCTION_CUPS.description: Suction cup appendages. Branch + 3x SoftTissue pads.

#VENOM_GLAND.description: Venom gland. 2x Crosslink (protein folding) + Muscle (injection mechanism).

#ELECTRORECEPTION.description: Electroreceptor organ. Feature-Sensor-Feature. Models ampullae of Lorenzini.

#LATERAL_LINE.description: Fish lateral line. Texture-Sensor-Texture. Models mechanoreceptor hair cell line.

ARTHROPOD_INSECT.description: 3-segment insect: head/thorax/abdomen, 3 limb pairs, optional wing pair
ARTHROPOD_INSECT.template: ATG CCG CCG CCG GTT GTT GTT #WING_PAIR #COMPOUND_EYE #ANTENNA TAA
rules: Contiguous Pro count (after ATG) = segment count
Val count = limb pair count (always bilateral)
Phe before Val = wing on that segment
Gly×2 + Arg at head = antenna pair

ARTHROPOD_CRUSTACEAN.description: Multi-segment crustacean with carapace, multiple limbs, claw pair
ARTHROPOD_CRUSTACEAN.template: ATG #CARAPACE GTT GTT GTT GTT GTT AAA AAA TCT TAA
rules: High Val count = more walking legs

VERTEBRATE_QUADRUPED.description: 4-limbed land vertebrate: torso, 2 limb pairs, head features
VERTEBRATE_QUADRUPED.template: ATG CCG CCG GTT GTT TTG #BIG_HORN TCT TAT TAA

VERTEBRATE_FISH.description: Streamlined fish body with fins along single axis
VERTEBRATE_FISH.template: ATG CCG TTG TTG #PECTORAL_FIN #DORSAL_FIN TTT CAA TCT ACT TAT TAA

VERTEBRATE_BIRD.description: Feathered bipedal bird: wing pair, bipedal legs, beak, plumage
VERTEBRATE_BIRD.template: ATG CCG GTT TTT TTT GTT AAA TCT ACT TAT #CREST TAA

VERTEBRATE_REPTILE.description: 4-limbed ectotherm with scales, long body, dragging tail
VERTEBRATE_REPTILE.template: ATG CCG CCG GTT GTT #DEWLAP TCT TCT TAA GCT GCT GCT TAA
rules: Post-STOP AAs can be suppressed or read as decorative

PLANT_FORB.description: Small flowering herb: upright stem, branching, flower
PLANT_FORB.template: ATG GCT GCT GCT GTT GTT TAT TAT TCT TGG TAA

PLANT_GRASS.description: Grass: tall stem, leaf blades, seed head
PLANT_GRASS.template: ATG GCT GCT GCT GCT TTG GAT GAT TTT TCT TAA

segmentationClock.title: The Segmentation Clock (Pro = segment)
segmentationClock.rule: Number of contiguous Pro (P) codons after ATG determines body segment count

examples.Crab: ATG CCG CCG CCG CCG CCG = 5 segments

bilateralSymmetry.title: Bilateral Symmetry Lock (Val = pair)
bilateralSymmetry.rule: Every Val (V) codon ALWAYS produces a bilateral (left+right) pair. No exceptions.

hoxGradient.title: HOX Positional Identity (position = anatomy)
hoxGradient.rule: Distance from ATG determines anatomical region: early = HEAD, middle = TORSO, late = TAIL

appendageIdentity.title: Appendage Identity Rule (Val + next = type)
appendageIdentity.rule: What codon FOLLOWS Val determines what body part grows on that branch

pigmentCascade.title: Pigmentation Cascade (Tyr-based real melanin pathway)
pigmentCascade.rule: Sequence of Tyr (Y) and Thr (T) codons before Tyr determines color output

testosterone.title: Androgen Amplifier (early Arg = bigger everything)
testosterone.rule: Arg (R) codons in the first 30% of the sequence amplify all subsequent morphemes

examples.CGT CGT early: Doubled horn size, brighter plumage, heavier body
